full_notes:
Manufacturing Industries is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins with its own framing, examples and questions, then builds the topic through source-backed facts. The notes below keep that order: first the opening problem, then the important terms, then the mechanisms, examples and exam-useful connections that appear in the chapter itself.

The text explains that 86 India : People and Economy Unit IV Unit IV Unit IV Unit IV Unit IV Chapter 8 INTERNATIONAL TRADE You have already studied about the various aspects of International trade in the book Fundamentals of Human Geography.

The text explains that international Trade is mutually beneficial as no country is self-sufficient.

The text explains that india’s international trade has undergone a sea change in recent years in terms of volume, composition as well as direction.

The text explains that although India’s contribution in the world trade is as low as one per cent of the total volume, yet it plays a significant role in the world economy.

The text explains that let us examine the changing pattern of India’s International trade.

The text explains that in 1950-51, India’s external trade was worth Rs.1,214 crore, which rose to Rs.

The text explains that can you calculate the percentage growth in 2020-21 over 1950-51?

The text explains that there are numerous reasons for this sharp rise in overseas trade, such as the momentum picked up by the manufacturing sectors, the liberal policies of the government and the diversification of markets.

The text explains that the nature of India’s foreign trade has changed over the years (Table 8.1).

The text explains that though there has been an increase in the total volume of import and export, the value of import continued to be higher than that of exports.

The text explains that 8.1 Extent of gap between Exports and Imports in India’s foreign trade During 2013-14 to 2021-22 Source: Economic Survey 2022-23

The text explains that international Trade 87 significant growth in the export.

The text explains that china and other East Asian countries are our major competitors.

The text explains that gems and jewellery contributes a larger share of India’s foreign trade.

The text explains that study Table 8.3 and select major commodities exported in 2021-22 and draw bar diagram.

The text explains that it has already started adopting suitable measures such as import liberalisation, reduction in import duties, delicensing and change from process to product patents.

The text explains that 90 India : People and Economy have been invited for the modernisation of ports in India.

The text explains that some of the Indian ports along with their hinterlands are as follows : Deendayal Port (Kandla port) situated at the head of Gulf of Kuchchh has been developed as a major port to cater to the needs of western and north western parts of the country and also to reduce the pressure at Mumbai port.

The text explains that the offshore terminal at Vadinar has been developed to reduce the pressure at the port.

The text explains that jawaharlal Nehru Port at Nhava Sheva was developed as a satellite port to relieve the pressure at the Mumbai port.

The text explains that it also handles fertilisers, petroleum products, edible Can you find out the reasons for the variations in the location of ports along the two coasts?

The text explains that in case of the major ports, the central government decides the policy and plays regulatory functions.

The text explains that the minor ports are there whose policy and functions are regulated by state governments.

The text explains that the British used the ports as suction points of the resources from their hinterlands.

The text explains that the extension of railways towards the interior facilitated the linking of the local markets to regional markets, regional markets to national markets and national markets to the international markets.

The text explains that it was expected that the country’s Independence will reverse the process, but the partition of the country snatched away two very important ports, i.e., Karachi port went to Pakistan and Chittagong port to the erstwhile east-Pakistan and now Bangladesh.

The text explains that most of the ports are equipped with modern infrastructure.

The text explains that previously, the development and modernisation was the responsibility of the government agencies, but considering the increase in function and need to bring these ports at par with the international ports, private entrepreneurs

The text explains that 92 India : People and Economy oils, coffee, tea, wood pulp, yarn, granite stone, molasses, etc.

The text explains that visakhapatnam Port in Andhra Pradesh is a land-locked harbour, connected to the sea by a channel cut through solid rock and sand.

The text explains that kamarajar (Ennore), a newly developed port in Tamil Nadu, has been constructed 25 km north of Chennai to relieve the pressure at Chennai port.

The text explains that chidambaranar (Tuticorin) Port was also developed to relieve the pressure of Chennai port.

The text explains that since 2017, under the UDAN scheme, a total number of 73 unserved/underserved airports including 9 Heliports and 2 water aerodromes have been operationalized (Source: PIB, Ministry of Civil Aviation, Govt. of India, 2023) You have already studied about the air transport in the previous chapter.

The text explains that you consult the chapter on transport to find out the main features of air transport in India.

The text explains that identify four cities where maximum number of air routes converge and also give reasons for this.

The text explains that 94 India : People and Economy EXERCISES EXERCISES EXERCISES EXERCISES EXERCISES 1.

The text explains that choose the right answers of the following from the given options.

The text explains that (i) Trade between two countries is termed as (a) Internal trade (c) International trade (b) External trade (d) Local trade (ii) Which one of the following is a land locked harbour?

The text explains that (a) Vishakhapatnam (c) Kamarajar (Ennore) (b) Mumbai (d) Haldia (iii) Most of India’s foreign trade is carried through (a) Land and sea (c) Sea and air (b) Land and air (d) Sea 2.

The text explains that (i) Mention the characteristics of India’s foreign trade.

The text explains that (iv) Name important items which India imports from different countries.

The text explains that (v) Name the ports of India located on the east coast.

Source: NCERT Class 12 India People and Economy, Chapter 8: Manufacturing Industries
